work.name: Purdue Aerospace Medicine VIP Team
highlights: Conducting research on blood transfusion methods in microgravity, contributing to a 7-member sub team focused on astronaut medical safety.
Designed a pressurized fluid delivery system for simulated microgravity transfusions across 10+ test cycles.
Developing and implementing an air bubble detection and removal system, reducing observable air inclusion with 400-500 ml at a 100-200 ml infusion rate.
Selected as a finalist in the 2026 NASA Human Lander Challenge, earning the opportunity to present the team's project at the NASA forum in Huntsville, Alabama.

work.name: Purdue Electric Vehicle Club
highlights: Developing a fully electric, driver-operated racing kart as part of a 10+ member engineering team for competition in the Electric Vehicle Grand Prix.
Engineering a high-efficiency split-axle drivetrain design, targeting improved torque distribution and reduced drivetrain losses compared to prior iterations.
Analyze race telemetry and strategy data across multiple practice sessions to optimize energy usage and race-day performance.

work.name: CircuitRunners Robotics – FIRST Robotics Challenge
work.position: Mechanical Team Member, Drive Team Coach
highlights: Served as drive team coach during the 2024 competition season, leading real-time strategy and driver coordination during 20+ matches.
Collaborated with mechanical, design, and electrical sub teams to design, prototype, and integrate dozens of custom robot components annually.
Trained 10+ new members per season on shop tool safety and guided them through the full engineering design, build, and test cycle processes.

work.name: Kennesaw State University Mechatronics Department
work.position: Student Intern with Senior Design Group
highlights: Collaborated with a 4-6 person senior design team to develop a wheelchair exoskeleton prototype aimed at assisting individuals with paraplegia.
Co-authored an IEEE-published research paper documenting the system's mechanical and electric engineering process.
Integrated CAD, motors, RC Link circuits, CAN communication, and Raspberry Pi control into a fully functional, biomechanically compatible prototype.

work.name: Eye Level Learning Center
highlights: Guided 40-60 students per shift, assigning homework and classwork, and grading papers.
Managed front desk operations including taking attendance, sorting schoolwork, and assisting parents.
